name: PRIMARY PULMONARY HYPERTENSION
definition: PLEXOGENIC PULMONARY ARTERIOPATHY = ppHTN idiopathic precapillary cause of pulmonary hypertension (pHTN)

demographics.Histo: plexogenic arteriopathy (HALLMARK) = intimal cell proliferation with focal disruption

dominant_finding: diameter of main pulmonary artery > 29 mm (87% sensitive, 89% specific) measured at
discriminating_features: diameter of main pulmonary artery > 29 mm (87% sensitive, 89% specific) measured at

CXR: right descending pulmonary artery > 25 mm wide
CT: diameter of main pulmonary artery > 29 mm (87% sensitive, 89% specific) measured at

small tortuous peripheral vessels = plexogenic arteriopathy
wall-adherent apposition thrombus in central pulmonary aa.
focal perivascular groundglass opacities in peripheral / perihilar distribution

hyperdense areas containing large caliber vessels
hypodense areas containing small caliber vessels

abnormal intravascular signal in 92% on gated T1WI ← slow arterial flow ← ↑ pulmonary
ANGIO: diffuse pattern of abruptly tapering + pruned subsegmental vessels

clinical.Rx: vasodilators, calcium channel blockers, diuretics, anticoagulants; lung / heart-lung
